articles.title: My friends left Ukraine after the Russian invasion. Now I wonder if we live in the same world | Diana Berg
articles.description: Anger, guilt, shame: the deepest unseen wound of this war is the rift between those who left and those who stayed behind “Can we reschedule?” Over the years of working with western colleagues, I had learned to accept that they don’t fully understand what war feels like from the inside. They would ask me to find someone in occupied Mariupol for an interview, or seem genuinely puzzled that I couldn’t just log on at a time that suited them. articles.title: Japan records its first ‘cruelly hot day’ as cities swelter in 40-degree heat
articles.description: In April Japan coined new term kokushobi for days above 40C as country advises workers to wear shorts to the office to cope with stifling heat Japan logged its first official kokushobi or “cruelly hot day” on Tuesday since the new term for when temperatures top 40 degrees Celsius was introduced in April. articles.description: A rare glimpse of a young puffin emerging from its nest has given conservationists fresh hope for the future of Dorset’s dwindling puffin population. The ‘puffling’ was spotted by volunteers working with the National Trust, who renewed their monitoring efforts at the site in March 2026 to understand why mainland England’s last breeding colony on the south coast was failing to produce chicks.
